title: branch names: “release-1.29.0” has uncommon zero
body:
The current branch names are
```console
$ git branch -la|grep rele
  remotes/origin/release-1.11-rhel
  remotes/origin/release-1.15
  remotes/origin/release-1.16
  remotes/origin/release-1.17
  remotes/origin/release-1.18
  remotes/origin/release-1.19
  remotes/origin/release-1.20
  remotes/origin/release-1.21
  remotes/origin/release-1.22
  remotes/origin/release-1.23
  remotes/origin/release-1.24
  remotes/origin/release-1.26
  remotes/origin/release-1.27
  remotes/origin/release-1.28
  remotes/origin/release-1.29.0
  remotes/origin/release-1.5-rhel
  remotes/origin/release-1.8
  remotes/origin/release-1.9-rhel
  remotes/origin/release-v1.14
````
In case it is not intentional to put a zero in `1.29.0`, I kindly ask you to remove the terminating zero from the brach name.

The zere is inconsitsent with the naming of the other branches.

comments.body: @dilyanpalauzov nice eagle eye and thanks for the issue.  That was indeed a mistake on my part.
